content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

May 13, 1985

Honorable Leon Panetta
Member of Congress
380 Alvarado Street
Monterey, CA 93940

Attention: Mary Becker

Re: Your Request for Advice on
Behalf of Joe Tanzi
Our No. A-85-107

Dear Congressman Panetta:

You have asked on behalf of your constituent, Mr. Joe
Tanzi, whether it is legal for an elected official such as a
city mayor in California to transfer campaign funds to another
candidate.

To our knowledge, there is currently no ban on the transfer
of contributions from one candidate to another, unless a local
contribution limitation ordinance is in effect in the
jurisdiction which might put a limit on such an activity. The
Political Reform Act, which this agency administers merely
requires that any such transfers be reported by the maker and by
the recipient (if $100 or more). (However, see proposal in
SB 118, enclosed.)

Outside the Political Reform Act, there are provisions in
the Elections Code (Sections 12400, et seq., copies enclosed)
which place certain restrictions on the "personal use" of
campaign funds. We are unable to advise you with respect to
these provisions as they are not contained in our Act. You may
wish to consult with the Office of the Attorney General if you
have questions regarding the Elections Code provisions.

If you have any questions regarding this letter, I may be
reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert B. Leidegh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh
Enclosures

LEON E. PANETTA
15TH DISTRICT, CALIFORNIA

WASHINGTON OFFICE:
339 CANNON HOUSE OFFICE BUILDING
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20515
(202) 225-2861

COMMITTEES:
BUDGET

DISTRICT OFFICES:
350 ALVARADO STREET
MONTEREY, CALIFORNIA 93940
(408) 649-3555

JOINT COMMITTEE
ON BUDGET PROCESS

HOLLISTER, CALIFORNIA
(408) 637-0600

AGRICULTURE
SUBCOMMITTEES:
DOMESTIC AGRICULTURAL, CONSUMER RELATIONS,
AND NUTRITION

MORRO BAY, CALIFORNIA
(805) 772-2028
SALINAS, CALIFORNIA
(408) 424-2229
SANTA CRUZ, CALIFORNIA
(408) 429-1976

HOUSE ADMINISTRATION
104 LEAVES

MAJORITY REGIONAL WHIP

Congress of the United States
House of Representatives
Washington, D.C. 20515

May 6, 1985

TO: Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95804

MAY 9 1985

ENCLOSURES FROM:
Joe Tanzi

RE:

Please review the enclosed. The constituent asks if it
is legal for an elected officials such as a city mayor, in
the State of California, to donate his/her campaign funds
to a candidate running for city council of the same or
of a different city. I thank you for your assistance in
this matter.

I would appreciate your attention to the attached correspondence.
Please direct your reply to the address below.

Thank you very much for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

LEON E. PANETTA
Member of Congress

Please respond to:
380 Alvarado Street
Monterey, California 93940
(408)649-3555

Attention: Mary Becker

B:   GENERAL INFORMATION REQUEST
b

DATE: __4/18/85__________

STAFF MEMBER __MLB______________ PHONE_X__VISIT____LETTER____

CONSTITUENT'S NAME______Mr. Joe Tanzi______________

ADDRESS:__________24 Wyndemere______________

__________Monterey, CA 93940______________

_________________________________________________

_________________________________________________

PHONE: __________/_624-8059______________
        area code

INFORMATION REQUESTED: (be specific)

Mr. Tanzi would like to know the legality of campaign funds on
the local level. He would like to know if the Mayor can give
campaign funds to Council Members.
